sourceTitle: Create & edit images with Notion AI

questions.question: Which Notion plan is required to access the image generation feature?

options.text: Free plan
options.explanation: Image generation is not available on the Free plan. It is restricted to Business and Enterprise plans.

options.text: Personal Pro plan
options.explanation: The Personal Pro plan does not support image generation features.

options.text: Business and Enterprise plans
options.explanation: Image generation is specifically listed as being available for Business and Enterprise plans only.

options.text: Team plan
options.explanation: While Business is mentioned, the Team plan is not listed as a requirement in the article.

questions.question: Can custom agents be used to generate images in Notion?

options.text: Yes, always
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that custom agents cannot generate images.

options.text: No, they cannot
options.explanation: The article lists 'Custom agents can’t generate images' as a limitation.

options.text: Only on Business plans
options.explanation: The restriction applies to all custom agents regardless of the plan.

options.text: Only in image blocks
options.explanation: The restriction applies to image generation in general, not specific to block types.

questions.question: When creating an image in an image block, what is the first step you must take?

options.text: Select the Notion AI option immediately
options.explanation: You must first create the image block by typing '/image' before you can select the AI option.

options.text: Type /image and press Enter
options.explanation: The first step in the process is to type '/image' on a page and press Enter.

options.text: Ask Notion AI to generate a random image
options.explanation: You must type the specific command '/image' first to open the block.

options.text: Hover over the block to select it
options.explanation: Hovering is used for editing existing images, not creating a new one.

questions.question: How does the Notion AI chat feature utilize the content of the current page when generating an image?

options.text: It ignores the page content completely
options.explanation: The article states that the chat 'uses key info from the page you're viewing'.

options.text: It uses key info from the page you're viewing
options.explanation: This allows the AI to create flowcharts or infographics based on the specific text described on the page.

options.text: It downloads the page as a PDF first
options.explanation: There is no mention of downloading the page as a PDF; it uses 'key info' directly.

options.text: It generates a random image unrelated to the text
options.explanation: The primary benefit of using chat for images is that it reflects the content on your page.

questions.question: What is the prerequisite action required before you can edit an existing image with AI?

options.text: Type /edit in the chat bar
options.explanation: Editing is done by hovering over the image directly, not by typing a command in the chat.

options.text: Select the 'Edit with AI' option
options.explanation: Selecting the option comes after hovering; hovering is the prerequisite step.

options.text: Hover over the image
options.explanation: The article lists 'Hover over an image in Notion' as the very first step in the 'Edit an existing image with AI' process.

options.text: Change the image block type to text
options.explanation: You don't need to change the block type; you just need to hover over the existing image.

questions.question: Is it possible to generate a new image within an image block on a mobile device?

options.text: Yes, on all devices
options.explanation: Mobile users are restricted to generating images only through Notion AI chat.

options.text: No, mobile users can only generate images in chat
options.explanation: The article notes a 'Note' that on mobile, you can generate images only through Notion AI chat (not in image blocks, or for cover images).

options.text: Yes, but only for cover images
options.explanation: The restriction applies to image blocks and cover images; mobile users can only use chat.

options.text: Only for Enterprise plan users on mobile
options.explanation: The restriction is based on device type (mobile), not specifically the plan, though the plan is required to use the feature.

questions.question: Which of the following prompts is best suited for converting a screenshot on a page into a polished diagram?

options.text: Create a flowchart for the process described on this page.
options.explanation: This prompt is better suited for creating a flowchart based on text descriptions rather than converting an existing screenshot.

options.text: Turn this screenshot into a polished diagram.
options.explanation: This prompt is explicitly listed in the article as an example of a chat command that works well for this specific task.

options.text: Make this look like a diagram.
options.explanation: While similar, the article specifically highlights the prompt 'Turn this screenshot into a polished diagram' as a recommended example.

options.text: Change the background to a snowy afternoon.
options.explanation: This prompt describes an edit to the background style rather than converting a screenshot to a diagram.

questions.question: If you receive an error message while trying to generate an image, which of the following could be the cause?

options.text: Your admin has disabled the feature
options.explanation: While this could prevent you from seeing the option, an error message usually implies a functional issue rather than a visibility one.

options.text: You have hit your daily or monthly usage limit
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'You may have hit your daily or monthly usage limit. Try again later.'

options.text: You are not using a Business or Enterprise plan
options.explanation: If you aren't on the right plan, you typically wouldn't see the option to generate at all, rather than getting an error.

options.text: The image block is too small
options.explanation: Image size is not mentioned as a cause for errors in the troubleshooting section.

questions.question: Who controls whether image generation is switched on for a workspace?

options.text: The end user
options.explanation: The article states that 'Workspace owners and admins control whether image generation is switched on'.

options.text: Notion Support
options.explanation: Notion Support does not control individual workspace settings; the owners and admins do.

options.text: Workspace owners and admins
options.explanation: This is the correct answer as stated directly in the first paragraph of the article.

options.text: Third-party developers
options.explanation: Third-party developers are not mentioned as having control over workspace-wide image generation settings.

questions.question: What is the recommended action to take immediately after generating an initial image?

options.text: Download the image to your computer
options.explanation: Downloading is an action you might take later, but the article recommends refining the prompt first to get the result right.

options.text: Share the image with your team
options.explanation: Sharing is not the immediate next step; refining the image is.

options.text: Refine the prompt until it looks right
options.explanation: The article instructs the user to 'Generate the image, then refine the prompt until it looks right'.

options.text: Delete the image block
options.explanation: You should not delete the image immediately; you should refine it to ensure it meets your needs.
